task_completion.visual_evidence: The WordPress Plugins page shows 'Yoast SEO' as active (usually indicated by a 'Deactivate' link instead of 'Activate', or a highlighted row), or the Yoast SEO menu ('Y SEO') is visible in the left-hand WordPress admin sidebar.

task_completion.description: Configure the site-wide Title Separator to a dash (-)
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Yoast SEO Settings (usually under Search Appearance or Site Basics), the Title Separator option is set to the dash symbol (-).
In the Yoast SEO Settings for the Homepage, the meta description field contains the text starting with 'Explore expert articles on WordPress development...'.

task_completion.description: Set the focus keyphrase for 'Getting Started with WordPress'
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the WordPress post editor for 'Getting Started with WordPress', the Yoast SEO meta box (usually at the bottom or in the sidebar) shows the 'Focus keyphrase' input field filled with 'getting started with wordpress'.

task_completion.description: Set the meta description for 'Getting Started with WordPress'
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the WordPress post editor for 'Getting Started with WordPress', the Yoast SEO meta box shows the 'Meta description' field filled with the text starting with 'Learn why WordPress powers over 40%...'.

task_completion.description: Set the focus keyphrase for '10 Essential WordPress Plugins Every Site Needs'
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the WordPress post editor for '10 Essential WordPress Plugins Every Site Needs', the Yoast SEO meta box shows the 'Focus keyphrase' input field filled with 'essential wordpress plugins'.

task_completion.description: Set the meta description for '10 Essential WordPress Plugins Every Site Needs'
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the WordPress post editor for '10 Essential WordPress Plugins Every Site Needs', the Yoast SEO meta box shows the 'Meta description' field filled with the text starting with 'Discover the top 10 must-have WordPress plugins...'.

integrity.description: Agent used the WordPress GUI to configure SEO settings
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent navigating the WordPress admin dashboard in a web browser, rather than using terminal commands (like wp-cli) or a database client to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual Yoast SEO interface
integrity.visual_evidence: The Yoast SEO meta boxes and settings pages should be genuinely rendered within the WordPress environment, not typed into a separate text editor or mocked.

integrity.description: Agent actually saved or updated the posts and settings
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for 'Post updated', 'Settings saved' notifications, or the 'Update'/'Save changes' buttons being clicked or showing a saved state in the screenshots.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Title Separator must visually be a dash (-). The Homepage meta description must contain the keywords: 'explore expert articles', 'development', 'blogging tips', and 'essential plugins'. For the post 'Getting Started with WordPress', the focus keyphrase is 'getting started with wordpress' and the meta description must contain: '40%', 'beginner', 'advantages', and 'first site'. For the post '10 Essential WordPress Plugins Every Site Needs', the focus keyphrase is 'essential wordpress plugins' and the meta description must contain: 'top 10', 'must-have', '2024', 'yoast seo', and 'supercharge'.
